title: pimpinella alpina
intro: Pimpinella alpina, also known as Alpine Anise or Small Wild Lovage, is a perennial plant in the carrot family native to the mountainous regions of Europe. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include its carminative, antiseptic, and diuretic properties. The health benefits of Pimpinella alpina include its ability to aid digestion, relieve respiratory issues, and reduce inflammation. Pimpinella alpina can be used in herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, making it a versatile and valuable addition to natural remedies.
